source_paper_title: On Averaging and Extrapolation for Gradient Descent
context:
Section 5.1, page 23 (PDF page 23/37)

"Numerically, we find much of our results on the benefits of extrapolation on the worst-case final objective gap carry over to the worst-case final gradient norm. However, the exact dual certificates v, λ, Z underlying our proof do not constitute proofs for the setting of gradient norms. We expect dual certificates proving a benefit from small extrapolations exist but have not identified their structure and hence leave proving such parallel results open."
title: Prove worst-case gradient-norm benefits of simple extrapolation for gradient descent
latex:
Let \(f:\mathbb{R}^m\to\mathbb{R}\) be convex and \(L\)-smooth (i.e., \(\nabla f\) is \(L\)-Lipschitz). Let \(x_\star\in\arg\min f\) exist and let \(x_0\in\mathbb{R}^m\) satisfy \(\|x_0-x_\star\|\le D\). Fix an integer \(N\ge 1\) and a constant stepsize \(h\in(0,1]\). Define the gradient descent iterates
\[
 x_{k+1}=x_k-\frac{h}{L}\nabla f(x_k),\qquad k=0,1,\dots,N-1,
\]
and for an extrapolation factor \(c\ge 1\) define the extrapolated output
\[
 x_\sigma := x_0 + c(x_N-x_0).
\]
The problem is to provide a rigorous worst-case analysis (tight up to constants, and ideally exact) for the gradient norm at the extrapolated point, namely to characterize the critical extrapolation range and worst-case value of
\[
\sup_{f\ \text{convex }L\text{-smooth}}\ \sup_{\|x_0-x_\star\|\le D}\ \|\nabla f(x_\sigma)\|,
\]
and in particular to prove (or refute) the existence of a nontrivial interval \([1,c_{\mathrm{crit}}]\) with \(c_{\mathrm{crit}}>1\) such that for all \(c\in[1,c_{\mathrm{crit}}]\) one has the tight bound
\[
\|\nabla f(x_\sigma)\|\le \frac{LD}{Nhc+1},
\]
with equality for some worst-case instance, and to determine the scaling of \(c_{\mathrm{crit}}\) (e.g., \(c_{\mathrm{crit}}=1+\Theta(1/N)\) as conjectured) and the associated worst-case functions/dual certificates in the performance-estimation-problem framework.
open_problem_quote: "Numerically, we find much of our results on the benefits of extrapolation on the worst-case final objective gap carry over to the worst-case final gradient norm. However, the exact dual certificates v, λ, Z underlying our proof do not constitute proofs for the setting of gradient norms. We expect dual certificates proving a benefit from small extrapolations exist but have not identified their structure and hence leave proving such parallel results open."

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
